html:
Exclusive to:

Breakfast Skillet is a meat food added in Don't Starve Together. It is cooked in a Crock Pot with 1 or more Eggs and 1 or more Vegetables and has a low priority value of 1.

Adding any kind of Meat will result in Pierogi. If a Twig is used as a filler, using any Meat item as the other filler will create Kabobs. Using a Tallbird Egg instead of a normal egg will instead create Tall Scotch Eggs.

Recipe

≥1

×2

Filler cannot be Meats and Dairy Product.

Examples

×2

×2

×2

Tips

  • Replacing any of the two filler ingredients with any kind of Meat will instead create Pierogi, a meal that provides the same amount of and , while restoring 20 more . Replacing the egg with a Tallbird Egg will create Tall Scotch Eggs, which heal over three times as much and four times as much . Given the abundance of Monster Meat, even in Winter, and the relative ease of collecting Tallbird Eggs, there is very little point in cooking Breakfast Skillets outside of fringe situations where Eggs and Vegetables are readily available, but one wishes to preserve their Meat supply.

Gallery

  • HD Breakfast Skillet as seen in the in-game Cookbook.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“Breakfast is the most scientific meal of the day.”
–Wilson
“Hey, there's vegetables hiding underneath!”
–Willow
“Egg with vegetables, a mighty breakfast!”
–Wolfgang
“A healthy diet for a long life. How dreadful.”
–Wendy
“THE SKILLET IS NOT ACTUALLY PART OF THE BREAKFAST”
–WX-78
“A healthy, balanced breakfast.”
–Wickerbottom
“Veggies? Did we run outta bacon and potatoes?”
–Woodie
“At least it's hot.”
–Maxwell
“Bah! The eggs have been spoiled by non-meats!”
–Wigfrid
“Stop trying to trick us into eating our veggies!”
–Webber
“The best way to start the day is with a hot breakfast.”
–Warly
“Friends help make belly stuff”
–Wormwood
“Less dishes to wash if you leave it in the pan.”
–Winona
“A mortal dish. Or should I say, a mortal pan.”
–Wortox
“Glurgh, eggy goop all over it!”
–Wurt
“Wow, it's so... healthy!”
–Walter
“Why waste time washing extra dishes when you can eat out of the pan?”
–Wanda

'''Breakfast Skillet''' is a [[meat]] [[food]] added in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. It is cooked in a [[Crock Pot]] with 1 or more [[Egg]]s and 1 or more [[Vegetable]]s and has a low priority value of 1.

Adding any kind of Meat will result in [[Pierogi]]. If a [[Twig]] is used as a filler, using any Meat item as the other filler will create [[Kabobs]]. Using a [[Tallbird Egg]] instead of a normal egg will instead create [[Tall Scotch Eggs]].

== {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Eggs
|item2 = Vegetables
|comparator2 = ≥
|count2 = 1
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Breakfast Skillet
|item3 = Filler
|count3 = 2
|note = Filler cannot be [[Meats]] and [[Dairy]] Product.
}}

== {{pic|32|Crock Pot}} Examples ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Egg
|item2 = Ripe Stone Fruit
|tool = Crock Pot
|count2 = 2
|result = Breakfast Skillet|item3=Twigs
}}
{{Recipe
|item1 = Egg
|item2 = Carrot
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Breakfast Skillet
|item3=Twigs|count3=2}}
{{Recipe
|item1 = Egg
|item2 = Carrot
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Breakfast Skillet
|item3 = Berries
|count3 = 2
}}
{{clear}}

=={{pic32|Prototype}} Tips==
* Replacing any of the two filler ingredients with any kind of Meat will instead create [[Pierogi]], a meal that provides the same amount of {{Hunger}} and {{Sanity}}, while restoring 20 more {{Hp}}. Replacing the egg with a [[Tallbird Egg]] will create [[Tall Scotch Eggs]], which heal over three times as much {{Hp}} and four times as much {{Hunger}}. Given the abundance of [[Monster Meat]], even in [[Seasons/Winter|Winter]], and the relative ease of collecting Tallbird Eggs, there is very little point in cooking Breakfast Skillets outside of fringe situations where Eggs and Vegetables are readily available, but one wishes to preserve their Meat supply.
